title: eliokarmos pendens
intro: Eliokarmos pendens, also known as "hanging dog's claw," is a type of flowering plant in the Bignoniaceae family. It is native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers are used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, with benefits including reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Eliokarmos pendens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or extracting their active compounds with a solvent like ethanol.
